heading: Relation to other proceedings or actions
label: 315 Relation to other proceedings or actions

children.heading: .—Inter partes review barred by civil action
children.label: 1 .—Inter partes review barred by civil action
children.text: An inter partes review may not be instituted if, before the date on which the petition for such a review is filed, the petitioner or real party in interest filed a civil action challenging the validity of a claim of the patent.

children.label: A
children.text: the patent owner moves the court to lift the stay;

children.label: B
children.text: the patent owner files a civil action or counterclaim alleging that the petitioner or real party in interest has infringed the patent; or

children.label: C
children.text: the petitioner or real party in interest moves the court to dismiss the civil action.

children.heading: .—Treatment of counterclaim
children.label: 3 .—Treatment of counterclaim
children.text: A counterclaim challenging the validity of a claim of a patent does not constitute a civil action challenging the validity of a claim of a patent for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: .—Patent Owner’s Action
children.label: b .—Patent Owner’s Action
children.text: An inter partes review may not be instituted if the petition requesting the proceeding is filed more than 1 year after the date on which the petitioner, real party in interest, or privy of the petitioner is served with a complaint alleging infringement of the patent. The time limitation set forth in the preceding sentence shall not apply to a request for joinder under subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Joinder
children.label: c .—Joinder
children.text: If the Director institutes an inter partes review, the Director, in his or her discretion, may join as a party to that inter partes review any person who properly files a petition under section 311 that the Director, after receiving a preliminary response under section 313 or the expiration of the time for filing such a response, determines warrants the institution of an inter partes review under section 314.

children.heading: .—Multiple Proceedings
children.label: d .—Multiple Proceedings
children.text: Notwithstanding sections 135(a), 251, and 252, and chapter 30, during the pendency of an inter partes review, if another proceeding or matter involving the patent is before the Office, the Director may determine the manner in which the inter partes review or other proceeding or matter may proceed, including providing for stay, transfer, consolidation, or termination of any such matter or proceeding.

children.heading: .—Proceedings before the office
children.label: 1 .—Proceedings before the office
children.text: The petitioner in an inter partes review of a claim in a patent under this chapter that results in a final written decision under section 318(a), or the real party in interest or privy of the petitioner, may not request or maintain a proceeding before the Office with respect to that claim on any ground that the petitioner raised or reasonably could have raised during that inter partes review.

children.heading: .—Civil actions and other proceedings
children.label: 2 .—Civil actions and other proceedings
children.text: The petitioner in an inter partes review of a claim in a patent under this chapter that results in a final written decision under section 318(a), or the real party in interest or privy of the petitioner, may not assert either in a civil action arising in whole or in part under or in a proceeding before the International Trade Commission under section 337 of the Tariff Act of 1930 that the claim is invalid on any ground that the petitioner raised or reasonably could have raised during that inter partes review.section 1338 of title 28
